operating_state: Consolidated revenues grew 14% to $92.4 billion driven by UnitedHealthcare revenue growth of 13% and Optum revenue growth of 22%. UnitedHealthcare served 1.5 million more people with growth across Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, and commercial offerings, partially offset by Medicaid redeterminations. Optum Health served 103 million people with organic growth in value-based care arrangements and business combinations. Optum Rx fulfilled 383 million adjusted scripts reflecting growth from new clients and existing client expansion.
cost_structure: Medical care ratio increased 70 basis points to 82.3% due to elevated outpatient care activity for seniors and business mix changes. Operating cost ratio increased 60 basis points to 15.0% primarily from business mix and investments to support future growth, partially offset by productivity advances. Operating margin remained flat at 9.2% as revenue growth offset cost pressures.
material_events: The Board increased quarterly dividend to $7.52 annually from $6.60 per share in June 2023. The company repurchased 13 million shares at an average price of $485.10 during the nine months, with authorization remaining for 18 million shares. Pending acquisitions totaling approximately $5 billion in consideration are subject to regulatory approval.
macro_exposures: Medicare Advantage rate pressures continue with 2024 Final Notice resulting in industry base rate decreases well below forward medical cost trends. Substantial risk adjustment model revisions will reduce funding for patients with greatest health challenges. Medicaid redeterminations across states impacted enrollment levels with partial offset from commercial growth.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued health care spending growth driven by inflation, medical technology advancement, and demographic trends. The company will continue seeking to balance growth and profitability while making adjustments to offset Medicare rate pressures including medical cost management, provider network changes, benefit adjustments, and premium implementations on a county-by-county basis.
uncertainty_profile: DOJ legal action relating to risk adjustment submission matter remains unresolved. Continued elevated senior outpatient care activity may persist in future periods. Medicare funding pressures create ongoing challenges requiring operational adjustments and strategic responses to maintain program viability and member benefits.
